title: Pipeline Registry Entry
description: Describes a named preprocessing pipeline (e.g. HCP minimal, fMRIPrep, CCS) at one or more released versions, with the parameter defaults intrinsic to each version. Governs files at kb/pipelines/{pipeline_id}.yaml. Consumed by the Configurator's base_pipeline.version inference path; import direction is one-way agent -> KB.

pipeline_id.description: Canonical lowercase slug (e.g. 'hcp_minimal', 'fmriprep', 'ccs'). Used as the kb/pipelines/{pipeline_id}.yaml filename stem.

display_name.description: Human-readable name, e.g. 'HCP Minimal Preprocessing Pipeline'.

aliases.description: Other names this pipeline is commonly known by, as authors phrase them in papers. Used for case-insensitive recognize() lookup. Should always include pipeline_id itself.

role.description: Role this tool plays in the pipeline.

component_tools.description: Empty-allowed hook for future linkage to kb/tools/{tool}/{version}.yaml CLI-flag inventories. Not consumed by Configurator in this build.

default_version.description: Optional. If set, resolve_version() returns this version with basis_type='version_default' and version_certain=true regardless of paper_date. Use only when the pipeline has a single canonical version users always invoke.

versions.description: Released versions of this pipeline. resolve_version() walks this list.

step_manifest.description: Optional structured description of the pipeline's preprocessing steps. MAY be stored, but is NEVER expanded into the agent's spec.steps (base pipelines stay opaque).

notes.description: Free-form notes about the pipeline as a whole.

version.description: Exact release version string as it appears in the upstream tag or changelog.

version_kind.description: Optional. How the version identifier should be interpreted. Inferable from release_source URL structure but explicit aids machine consumers.

release_source.description: URL to the upstream release tag, changelog entry, or Zenodo deposit.

param_defaults.description: Preprocessing parameter defaults intrinsic to this pipeline at this version. Keys are dotted spec field paths (e.g. 'spatial_normalization.target_space'). Only fields the pipeline pins at this version are populated; missing fields signal 'this pipeline does not pin this parameter at this version'.

value.description: Default value: a scalar/array, the not_applicable or needs_verification sentinel, or a conditional_default derived from a sibling extracted field. Never null.

proposed_confidence.description: KB's proposed confidence. The Configurator clamps to BASIS_CEILINGS['version_default']=0.95 before emitting an InferredDefault. Required for scalar/sentinel values; for a conditional_default the per-rule proposed_confidence carries this instead.

source.description: Citation, e.g. 'Glasser et al. 2013, fig. 1' or 'HCP Pipelines v3.0.0 release notes'. Required for scalar/sentinel values; for a conditional_default the per-rule source carries this instead. No fabrication.

notes.description: Free-form notes (e.g. caveats from the source).

allOf.comment: A conditional_default (an object with `conditional_on`) carries proposed_confidence + source PER RULE, so they are optional at entry level. Every other value shape (scalar, array, or a sentinel object without `conditional_on`) REQUIRES entry-level proposed_confidence + source. The `type: object` guard is load-bearing: without it, `required` is vacuously true on non-object (scalar) values and the branch misfires.

not_applicable_sentinel.description: Sentinel for 'this pipeline at this version explicitly does NOT apply this step' (e.g. HCP minimal does not perform temporal filtering). Decodes to the NotApplicable singleton on the Python side; Configurator translates this to LeftMissing on the spec side with an informative reason (couple_stages forbids inference-side NotApplicable when extraction is MISSING/DEFERRED).

todo.description: Where to look to verify the value.

needs_verification_sentinel.description: Sentinel for 'we know this pipeline has a default at this version but the exact value isn't verified yet'. get_param_defaults() OMITS these from its return dict, so no InferredDefault fires. Forces a structured TODO surface.

conditional_on.description: Dotted spec-field path of the SIBLING extracted field this default is derived from (e.g. 'surface_projection.target_surface'). The Configurator reads its EXTRACTED value to select a rule; if that field is MISSING no inference fires.

when.description: The conditional_on field value(s) selecting this rule. String or list-of-strings (matches any).

value.description: The derived default value when a `when` matches. No sentinels inside a rule.

source.description: Prose: code-anchor + verified-vs-inferred grain. No fabrication.

conditional_default.description: A default DERIVED from a sibling extracted field's value. Resolved consumer-side (Configurator) against the paper's extracted data - the KB stays paper-agnostic. Maps to DerivedBasis (ceiling 0.70) on the agent side; the B0 code-verified-vs-lineage-inferred asymmetry is carried by per-rule proposed_confidence + source, not a source_type enum.
